details.query: O'Reilly book cover titled Hands-On Large Language Models by Jay Alammar and Maarten Grootendorst with subtitle Language Understanding and Generation
diagram showing The Transformer block with red flame logo converting input Je suis étudiant to output I am a student
Diagram showing Encoders connecting to Decoders to translate input "Je suis étudiant" into output "I am a student"
transformer diagram with 6 stacked encoders and decoders translating Je suis étudiant input into I am a student output
Diagram of a simplified transformer encoder block containing self-attention feeding into a feed forward neural network.
diagram showing encoder connecting to the encoder-decoder attention layer between decoder self-attention and feed forward layers
diagram showing input embedding vectors x1, x2, and x3 for the French words Je, suis, and étudiant
Encoder diagram mapping Je suis étudiant inputs x1 x2 x3 to z1 z2 z3 vectors via self-attention and feed forward layers
Transformer encoder diagram showing inputs Thinking and Machines labeled x1 x2 passing through self-attention to z1 z2 and feed forward layers to r1 r2
Visualization showing Layer 5 Input-Input attention weights connecting 'The_' and 'animal_' to the token 'it_' in a sentence
diagram showing Thinking Machines embeddings X1 X2 generating q k v vectors using weight matrices WQ WK WV
Self-attention diagram showing inputs Thinking and Machines calculating score q1 dot k1 equals 112
self-attention calculation for Thinking and Machines showing scores 112 and 96 divided by 8 before softmax
Self-attention calculation for words Thinking and Machines showing q1 dot k1 equals 112 divided by 8 yielding softmax 0.88
diagram showing input matrix X multiplied by weights WQ, WK, and WV to calculate Q, K, and V matrices
diagram visualizing scaled dot-product attention equation with softmax of Q times K transpose over square root d_k multiplied by V yielding Z
diagram showing Thinking Machines input X and weight matrices generating Q K V for Attention Head #0 and #1
input X containing Thinking Machines calculating attention separately in eight heads producing output matrices Z0 through Z7
diagram showing concatenated attention heads Z0 through Z7 multiplied by weight matrix WO to produce Z matrix for FFNN
Diagram showing input Thinking Machines X producing 8 heads of Q K V matrices and concatenated Z output
Layer 5 Input-Input attention visualization showing "it_" attending to "animal_" and "tire" to resolve coreference in transformers
Layer 5 Input-Input attention visualization showing weights connecting The animal to the highlighted token it_
Diagram illustrating positional encoding adding t1 t2 t3 to embeddings for input "Je suis étudiant" before entering ENCODER #0
diagram adding positional encoding vectors to embeddings x1, x2, x3 for inputs Je, suis, étudiant
sinusoidal positional encoding heatmap with embedding dimensions 0 to 510 and sequence positions 0 to 19
sinusoidal positional encoding heatmap showing frequency decreasing along depth dimension with alternating sine-cosine vertical stripes
Encoder #1 diagram with inputs x1 Thinking and x2 Machines passing through self-attention and add & normalize blocks
Encoder #1 diagram with inputs Thinking and Machines showing self-attention and LayerNorm(X + Z) residual calculation
Transformer diagram showing inputs Thinking and Machines with Encoder #2 output connected to Decoder #1 encoder-decoder attention
diagram showing decoder stack output transforming into logits and log_probs, using argmax to select index 5 and the word 'am'
Output Vocabulary table mapping words 'a', 'am', 'I', 'thanks', 'student' to indices ending with <eos> token
output vocabulary table mapping word am to index 1 and one-hot encoding vector showing 1.0 at second position
Untrained Model Output with 0.2 probabilities versus Correct and desired output 1.0 for token thanks
Target Model Outputs diagram showing one-hot encoded matrix for the sequence "I am a student <eos>" across positions #1 to #5
trained model output heatmap showing 0.93 probability for "I" at position #1 and 0.98 for "<eos>" at position #5
diagram showing BERT semi-supervised training to predict masked words followed by supervised spam classification
BERT model processing "Help Prince Mayuko" input via feed-forward classifier and softmax to predict 85% spam
visual comparison of BERTBASE and BERTLARGE model sizes represented by scaled yellow character illustrations
diagram comparing BERT BASE with 12 stacked ENCODER layers versus BERT LARGE with 24 ENCODER layers
BERT diagram showing input tokens [CLS] Help Prince Mayuko flowing to outputs numbered 1 to 512
BERT diagram showing inputs [CLS] Help Prince Mayuko feeding into a stack of 12 encoders
BERT diagram showing 12 stacked encoder layers processing input tokens [CLS] Help Prince Mayuko ending at position 512
BERT using [CLS] vector and feed-forward neural network plus softmax classifier to predict 85% Spam for "Help Prince Mayuko"
VGG-16 diagram showing mostly feature extraction layers and FC-4096 classification layers predicting 95% Egyptian cat
horizontal embedding vector displaying specific numerical values -0.34, -0.84, 1.04, and -1.17 in a single row
horizontal row of four light blue squares representing a word embedding vector in transformer visualization
Elmo meme explaining contextual word embeddings for stick using the specific sentence stick to improvisation in this skit
ELMo diagram showing "Words to embed" for "Let's stick to improvisation in this skit" flowing to vectors
Diagram where 'Let's stick to' embeddings pass through stacked LSTM layers and FFNN + Softmax to predict 'Improvisation'
diagram of forward and backward language models processing 'Let’s stick to' through two LSTM layers for embedding step #1
ELMo embedding diagram step 2 displaying hidden layers concatenated and multiplied by task weights s0 s1 s2
diagram identifying OpenAI Transformer with flame icon as composed of DECODERS
stack of 12 decoder layers processing 'Let's stick to' through FFNN + Softmax to predict 'Improvisation'
OpenAI Transformer diagram processing input "Help Prince Mayuko" via FFNN + Softmax to output 85% Spam
Diagram showing Classification, Entailment, and Similarity tasks using Start, Delim, and Extract tokens feeding into Transformer and Linear layers
BERT diagram showing randomly masking 15% of tokens where FFNN + Softmax predicts 'Improvisation' from the masked position
BERT diagram predicting likelihood that Sentence B belongs after Sentence A using [CLS] token output and FFNN + Softmax
diagram comparing BERT fine-tuning architectures for MNLI Class Label prediction versus SQuAD v1.1 Start/End Span prediction
BERT diagram showing 12 stacked ENCODER layers generating contextualized embeddings for inputs Help Prince Mayuko
Chart comparing contextualized embedding strategies for "Help" showing Concat Last Four Hidden layers achieves best 96.1 F1 score
diagram showing text prompt "paradise cosmic beach" entering Stable Diffusion model to generate a tropical scene
diagram showing pirate ship text and landscape image inputs feeding into Stable Diffusion to generate composite output

Stable Diffusion diagram showing text input "paradise cosmic beach" feeding into Text Encoder and Image Generator
Stable Diffusion diagram showing text encoder and image generator block containing image information creator feeding into image decoder
Stable Diffusion diagram displaying Image Information Creator block with UNet and Scheduler between CLIPText and Autoencoder decoder
Stable Diffusion diagram showing CLIPText encoder outputting 77x768 embeddings for Image Information Creator UNet and Scheduler
Stable Diffusion diagram showing Text Encoder (CLIPText) feeding token embeddings to Image Information Creator (UNet + Scheduler) to process random image information tensors
Stable Diffusion diagram showing Image Information Creator processing random image information tensor through 50 UNet steps
diffusion diagram showing UNet Steps 1 to 50 feeding an Image Decoder (Autoencoder decoder) labeled Image Information Creator
forward diffusion diagram showing "Noise sample 1" and step 3 "Pick an amount" selecting level 1 intensity for a pyramid image
diagram illustrating forward diffusion generating a 2nd training example using noise sample 2 and picking noise amount 2
Diagram mapping input noise amount and noisy image to output noise sample labels for a UNet noise predictor
UNet training step diagram showing noise amount 3 input generating Unet Prediction compared to Actual noise label
diagram showing Trained Noise Predictor (UNet) using Noise amount: 3 and input to generate Predicted noise sample
Reverse diffusion step 1 diagram showing trained noise predictor UNet using noise amount 3 to subtract predicted noise sample
Reverse diffusion diagram where a noise predictor uses noise amounts 1 and 2 to subtract predicted noise samples.
diagram showing Original image passing through Image Encoder and central grid to Image Decoder producing Generated image
image encoder outputting compressed latent image and training samples with specific noise amounts 1, 2, and 3
Image Information Creator using UNet Step 50 to denoise complete noise before feeding the processed information to the Image Decoder
Latent diffusion diagram showing semantic map and text conditioning passing through tau theta to U-Net QKV blocks
FID-10K versus CLIP Score Pareto curves for T5 encoders and DrawBench alignment and fidelity comparison bars
Dataset figure showing IMAGE and CAPTION rows with specific samples like 'Soaring by Peter Eades' and 'Far Cry 4 concept art'
Diagram labeled 1 Embed image and text showing CLIP Image Encoder and Text Encoder processing Image 1 and Caption 1
CLIP diagram showing Image and Text Encoders comparing embeddings for Prediction 1 Similar vs 0 Not similar
diagram showing CLIP step 3 update the models with dotted lines flowing from label similarity to encoders
diagram showing Noise Predictor UNet with Text Conditioning taking noise amount 3 and prompt text information to output predicted noise sample
Dataset diagram mapping inputs of step image and text to noise sample labels for a Noise Predictor UNet with Text Conditioning
diagram showing noisy compressed image latents and noise amount 3 feeding into a Noise Predictor UNet to output a predicted noise sample
"Noise Predictor UNet diagram showing noise amount embedding feeding into ResNet blocks alongside noisy compressed image latents"
Noise Predictor UNet processing Noisy compressed image latents, Text information, and Noise amount 3 to output a Predicted noise sample
